judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit a/b/c equally well, but only Report 2 identifies the missing environment-level gate and recommends ANDing it with the per-tenant flag (expectation d), giving it a fuller answer overall.

report_1.evidence: Reminders are sent automatically — the feature defaults to on, not off. The decision is made in backend/app/feature_flags.py, function auto_send_reminders_enabled, lines 10–13... if so, line 12 returns True.

report_2.evidence: Reminders are sent automatically. The decision is made in auto_send_reminders_enabled at backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13... when the auto_send_reminders key is absent... the function returns True.

report_1.evidence: Where I'd change it: backend/app/feature_flags.py:12 — change return True to return False (equivalently, rewrite the function body as return bool(tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders", False))), so a missing/unset value defaults to disabled

report_2.evidence: I would change backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12, inverting the None-branch to return False... With that change, an unset flag now means "off" on both the backend (no messages sent) and the frontend

report_1.evidence: the two flags are not equivalent in risk (cosmetic UI preference vs. outbound patient communication with cost/consent implications), and the same commit's own frontend code defaults the visible toggle to the opposite state

report_2.evidence: compact_calendar_view_enabled and auto_send_reminders_enabled sit in different risk categories (a cosmetic UI preference vs. an automated action that contacts patients), and the default that's correct for one is wrong for the other.

Add an env-level master switch / kill switch. infra/main.tf defines only a generic environment variable — there is no global toggle to disable reminders across all tenants at once if something goes wrong post-launch. Per project documentation's recipe for new flags, a per-tenant automated-messaging flag should be ANDed with an env-level master, and a rollback affordance should be defined before shipping the "on" state.
